message:
style(core): Apply rustfmt formatting to context, middleware, and request modules

Apply consistent code formatting across three core modules to comply
with project style guidelines (cargo fmt).

context.rs:

- Collapse single-expression match arm for CancelledError into a
  single line, removing unnecessary braces around the Outcome::Cancelled
  return value

middleware.rs:

- Reformat Handler::call() trait method signature from multi-line
  parameter layout to two-line format with return type on second line
- Break long method chains in MiddlewareStack::handle() into multi-line
  format with .await on its own line for improved readability:
  - run_after_hooks() call in Break arm
  - run_after_hooks() call after handler.call()

request.rs:

- Break Headers::get() method chain across multiple lines:
  self.inner.get(...).map(...)
- Break Headers::insert() method chain across multiple lines:
  self.inner.insert(..., ...)

All changes are whitespace-only formatting adjustments with no
behavioral modifications. Code logic and semantics are unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
